description: Fix bugs, resource leaks, dead code, and missing guards discovered during a thorough audit of the MCP server backend. Covers event-loop-blocking spawnSync calls, dead error-handling code in adapter file cleanup, config save mutex that silently swallows write errors, missing network capture cleanup in analyze-site, and Windows compatibility for npm spawns.

userStories.title: Replace event-loop-blocking spawnSync in version-check.ts with async spawn
userStories.description: As a platform user, I want the MCP server to remain responsive during plugin update checks so that tool calls and health checks are not blocked for 10+ seconds while npm queries run.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchLatestVersion uses async child_process.spawn (or execFile) instead of spawnSync
checkForUpdates is async and runs npm view queries concurrently with Promise.allSettled
The server event loop is never blocked by version checking
fetchLatestVersion also uses platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm' string for Windows compatibility
Existing unit tests in version-check.test.ts pass (update mocks from spawnSync to the new async spawn pattern)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.ts. The function `fetchLatestVersion` (line 25) uses `spawnSync('npm', ['view', ...])` which blocks the Node.js event loop. `checkForUpdates` (line 79) calls this sequentially for each npm plugin. With 5 plugins and 2s registry latency each, the server is completely unresponsive for ~10s. This is called from `performReload` (platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts line 379) which is already async.

Additionally, line 27 uses bare `spawnSync('npm', ...)` without `platformExec('npm')` — on Windows, `npm` must be invoked as `npm.cmd` via `platformExec`. Compare with platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts line 163 which correctly uses `platformExec('npm')`.

The `checkForUpdates` caller in reload.ts (line 378-382) needs to become `await checkForUpdates(state)`. The function signature changes from sync to async. Update the test file (platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.test.ts) to mock the new async spawn instead of spawnSync.

userStories.title: Replace event-loop-blocking spawnSync in searchNpmPlugins with async spawn
userStories.description: As a platform user, I want the MCP server to remain responsive during plugin search requests so that all other WebSocket messages and HTTP requests continue to be handled.
acceptanceCriteria: searchNpmPlugins in plugin-management.ts is async and uses the existing spawnAsync helper instead of spawnSync
handlePluginSearch in extension-handlers.ts awaits the now-async searchNpmPlugins
The server event loop is never blocked during npm search
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts line 151. `searchNpmPlugins` uses `spawnSync(platformExec('npm'), ['search', ...])` which blocks the event loop. The same file already has an async `spawnAsync` helper (line 64) and `runNpmGlobal` (line 97) that demonstrate the correct async pattern with timeout. Convert `searchNpmPlugins` to use `spawnAsync` with a timeout (e.g., 30s) similar to `runNpmGlobal`.

The caller chain: extension-protocol.ts line 401 calls `handlePluginSearch` (extension-handlers.ts line 489) which calls `searchNpmPlugins` (line 501). `handlePluginSearch` needs to become async and the call in extension-protocol.ts needs `await`. Check that the dispatch handler in extension-protocol.ts (around line 401) properly awaits the result.

userStories.title: Fix dead error-handling code in adapter file cleanup functions
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want adapter file deletion failures to be properly detected and logged so that stale files don't silently accumulate on disk.
acceptanceCriteria: cleanupStaleAdapterFiles: the .catch(() => {}) is removed from unlink calls inside Promise.allSettled, so rejections are properly captured by allSettled
cleanupStaleExecFiles: same fix — remove .catch(() => {}) from unlink calls inside Promise.allSettled
writeAdapterFile old-file cleanup: same fix — remove .catch(() => {}) from unlink calls inside Promise.allSettled
The warning log on rejection in cleanupStaleAdapterFiles (line 137) is now reachable
cleanupStaleExecFiles log accurately reports the count of successfully deleted files (not total attempted)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/adapter-files.ts. Three locations have the same pattern: `Promise.allSettled(files.map(f => unlink(...).catch(() => {})))`. The `.catch(() => {})` converts all rejections into fulfilled promises with `undefined`, making the `allSettled` result always 'fulfilled' — so the `result.status === 'rejected'` check is dead code.

Line 74: `writeAdapterFile` old file cleanup — remove `.catch(() => {})`. If you want to continue ignoring individual failures here, use `Promise.all(...).catch(() => {})` at the outer level instead, or just let allSettled handle it.

Line 132: `cleanupStaleAdapterFiles` — remove `.catch(() => {})` so the warning on line 137 becomes reachable.

Line 219: `cleanupStaleExecFiles` — remove `.catch(() => {})` and update the log message on line 220 to count actual successes (filter `result.status === 'fulfilled'`) rather than logging total count.

Existing tests in adapter-files.test.ts may need updates if they rely on the current behavior.

userStories.title: Fix config save mutex that silently swallows write errors
userStories.description: As a platform user, I want config save failures to be propagated to callers so that I'm notified when my configuration changes fail to persist.
acceptanceCriteria: When atomicWriteConfig fails (e.g., disk full), the caller of saveConfig receives the rejection
When atomicWriteConfig fails, the caller of saveToolConfig receives the rejection
The mutex chain does not deadlock after a failed write — subsequent writes proceed normally
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts lines 303-318 (saveConfig) and 330-363 (saveToolConfig).

The bug: `state.configWriteMutex` is assigned to the `.catch()` chain. Inside the `.catch()` handler, `state.configWriteMutex` is reset to `Promise.resolve()` (line 313), then the error is re-thrown. Due to microtask ordering, when the `await state.configWriteMutex` on line 317 executes, `state.configWriteMutex` has already been reassigned to `Promise.resolve()` by the catch handler — so the `await` resolves successfully even though the write failed. The re-thrown error from the `.catch()` becomes an unhandled rejection.

Fix approach: Capture the promise chain in a local variable and await that, rather than re-reading the mutable `state.configWriteMutex`. For example:
```
const writePromise = (async () => { await prev; ... })();
state.configWriteMutex = writePromise.catch(() => { /* reset mutex only */ state.configWriteMutex = Promise.resolve(); });
await writePromise; // This preserves the rejection for the caller
```
Apply the same fix to both `saveConfig` and `saveToolConfig`. Check config.test.ts for tests that may cover the error path.

userStories.title: Add network capture cleanup to analyze-site error path
userStories.description: As a platform user, I want the analyze-site tool to clean up network capture resources even when intermediate steps fail so that CDP debugger sessions don't leak.
acceptanceCriteria: disableNetworkCapture is called in the finally block of analyzeSite, before closeTab, wrapped in its own try-catch
If getNetworkRequests throws, network capture is still disabled before the tab is closed
The existing happy-path behavior is unchanged

userStories.title: Fix analyze-site race: enable network capture before page navigation
userStories.description: As a platform user, I want analyze-site to capture early page-load network requests (auth token exchanges, initial data fetches) so that API and auth detection is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: analyzeSite opens the tab to about:blank first (or uses a similar strategy to delay navigation)
Network capture is enabled before the target URL is loaded
Early page-load API calls are captured by the network monitor
The existing detection modules and wait behavior work unchanged
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts lines 807-833.

Currently: openTab(url) at line 813, then enableNetworkCapture at line 827. Requests made between tab open and capture enable are lost.

Fix approach: Open the tab to `about:blank` instead of the target URL, enable network capture on the blank tab, then navigate to the target URL using `browser.navigateTab`. This ensures the CDP debugger is attached before any requests fire.

Change:
1. Line 813: `dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.openTab', { url: 'about:blank' })` 
2. After enableNetworkCapture (line 830): add `await dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.navigateTab', { tabId, url })`
3. The wait timer (line 833) should start after navigation, not after capture enable
